task_completion.description: Verify the 'Sample Order Lines' dataset is loaded into the project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dataset name 'Sample Order Lines' is visible in the Data panel on the left side of the Oracle Analytics Desktop interface.

task_completion.description: Verify a calculated data element named 'Day of Week' has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The element 'Day of Week' is visible in the Data panel (usually with a calculation/fx icon), or the 'Add Calculation' dialog is shown with the name 'Day of Week' extracting the day from 'Order Date'.

task_completion.description: Verify a Heatmap visualization has been created on the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A heatmap chart (a grid of colored rectangular cells) is visible on the main canvas.

task_completion.description: Verify the Heatmap uses 'Month' for rows and 'Day of Week' for columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Grammar panel, 'Month' is placed in the Category (Y-Axis/Rows) section and 'Day of Week' is placed in the X-Axis/Columns section, or the chart axes are explicitly labeled with these elements.

task_completion.description: Verify the Heatmap values represent order volume.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Grammar panel, a metric such as 'Count of Order ID' or 'Row Count' is placed in the Color or Values section for the heatmap.

task_completion.description: Verify the visualization is titled 'Order Volume Heatmap'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Order Volume Heatmap' is visible as the title directly above the heatmap visualization on the canvas.

task_completion.description: Verify the workbook is saved as 'Seasonality_Analysis'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The application window title bar, the top toolbar, or the save dialog displays the name 'Seasonality_Analysis' (or 'Seasonality_Analysis.dva').

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Oracle Analytics Desktop GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Oracle Analytics Desktop interface (menus, drag-and-drop, calculation editor) rather than terminal commands, scripts, or external file manipulation.

integrity.description: Verify the heatmap is genuinely rendered by the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The heatmap appears integrated within the Oracle Analytics canvas, matching the configurations shown in the Grammar and Data panels, rather than being an imported image.

integrity.description: Verify the 'Day of Week' column was created via the application's calculation features.
integrity.visual_evidence: The presence of the custom calculation icon (fx) next to 'Day of Week' in the data panel or the calculation editor dialog, indicating it was not just a pre-existing column in a modified source file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is 'Seasonality_Analysis.dva'. The visualization must be a 'heatmap'. The required columns for the visualization are 'Day of Week' (which must be a calculated element) and 'Month'.
